title: After-Action Report — Career Leaderboard Placement + Stat-Invariant Repair
scope: Two threads ran back-to-back: 1. Career leaderboard placement. A multi-season career-stats leaderboard had been built in the web app; the user chose the almanac as its home. Move it there, remove the interim web-app page, and prove it with real data. 2. "Fix the bugs and errors only." The tests/test_stat_invariants.py harness was failing 6 of its checks against a multi-season sim. Turn it green.

rationale.item: The career data layer (snapshot tables season_player_batting / season_player_pitching, written at archive time) was already in place and is the right home for cross-season totals — raw game logs are wiped each season, so career accumulation must read the snapshots.
Moved the presentation into the almanac so it lives with the rest of the stat site.

delegation_events.decision: Almanac chosen as the home for the career leaderboard
delegation_events.quote: A multi-season career-stats leaderboard had been built in the web app; the user chose the **almanac** as its home.

delegation_events.decision: Scope limited to fixing bugs/errors in invariants

actions.description: Almanac loader loads the two snapshot tables via the existing _try_query graceful-degradation path
compute.py aggregates snapshots per player into ranked career batting/pitching leaderboards (_career_leaderboards) attached to Views.career; rate stats gate on a career minimum (50 PA / 60 outs)
New Career page renders in both static export (career.html) and live blueprint (/career.html), with a nav entry
Removed the interim web-app /seasons/career route, helpers, template, and the seasons-page link
Fixed stale test #8: check xra instead of dead xfip key
Fixed stale test #5: derive W straight from _pitcher_wl_map and keep W ≤ G and ΣW == decided-games checks
Phase-outs cap fix (#1, #4): single authoritative GameState.out_cap() reused by is_half_over; _record_out refuses to record once the cap is reached
Phantom HR out fix: strip all out-indices from any hr/home_run outcome at the point the contact event is emitted
Two-way reconciliation: replace the leftover block with a two-way reconciliation to state.outs — trim the batter's own structured charge first, then peel back TOA runner credits LIFO
Declaration jump fix: treat the declaration event as zero engine outs in the reconciliation
Pitcher spell drop fix: keep any spell with pitcher_outs_this_spell > 0
Joker override leak fix: clear state.batter_override at the start of every half (run_half)
Steal-into-occupied-base fix (#9): a runner cannot steal an occupied base — the generator skips such attempts and the handler waves them off
Walk-Back engine guard: a bonus runner only counts as a Walk-Back run when a run was actually booked on the current spell (pitcher_runs_this_spell >= 1); otherwise tick wb_faced but not wb_runs

risk_posture: Reverted the change rather than ship a no-op.
Reverted, because it changes ER system-wide for a result that's still red.
Held commits until verified. Engine changes that touch every box score were not committed before a fresh-sim harness run confirmed them.
It is a principled engine fix (it makes the path's long-standing documented assumption explicit), not an extraction-level clamp.

validation_claims.claim: Ran a real 5-season carry-forward history; counting leaders showed seasons=5 with totals ≈ 5× a single season, confirming roster continuity and snapshot accumulation
validation_claims.check_method: Rebuild a 5-season history DB and inspect the career boards

validation_claims.claim: All 10 checks in tests/test_stat_invariants.py pass on a freshly-built single-season DB (~2466 games)
validation_claims.check_method: Build a season DB and run pytest tests/test_stat_invariants.py

validation_claims.claim: Full-season spot-checks: batter_outs == opp_pitcher_outs for every (game, team, phase); SUM(wb_runs) <= SUM(unearned_runs) for every (game, team); er_arc1+er_arc2+er_arc3 == er and no er < 0 / unearned_runs > runs_allowed rows
validation_claims.check_method: Run the same SQL spot-checks against a freshly built season DB

validation_claims.claim: No regressions: o27/ (54) and o27v2/ (61) suites stay green
Invariants 1 and 4 went green; engine unit tests unaffected
validation_claims.check_method: Run invariant suite and engine unit tests

non_actions.item: Pitcher-side spell-drop fix (hypothesis A) was reverted
non_actions.reason: a re-sim showed the aggregate gap unchanged and it added a few reverse mismatches

non_actions.item: Two-way reconciliation in render.py (first attempt) reverted
non_actions.reason: On a full re-sim the gap was unchanged (≈3060 mismatched team-halves) — reverted the change rather than ship a no-op
it changes ER system-wide for a result that's still red

non_actions.item: Extraction-level team-aggregate reconciliation for #9 not pursued
non_actions.reason: would make the invariant green but only approximates per-pitcher attribution, so it was not pursued

residual_risks: Career totals only accrue from seasons archived after the snapshot tables existed
The career rate-stat minimums are low enough that a small-sample season can top a rate board (a tuning knob, not a bug)

A full-season rebuild surfaced one residual wb_runs > unearned group — proof there was at least one more base-erase path beyond the steal (closed via the engine guard rather than hunting every rare erase path)
The sim is not reproducible across processes; the archive build is not the one-shot loop — the last residual usually hides in the build-only population
open_hooks: Next attempt should instrument _update_stats with an independent engine-out counter (note: superseded by the UPDATE (FIXED) section)
Career rate-stat minimums are a tuning knob
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Career Leaderboard Placement + Stat-Invariant Repair
Stale tests (the test drifted from production)
Real bug — batter/pitcher out reconciliation (#2, #3) — OPEN
Real bug — walk-back run classification (#9) — OPEN
unmapped_content: What went well: Query the data, don't trust the message (game 9875 vs the 27-out continuous + walk-off model settled by actual rows); Single source of truth (out_cap() replaced duplicated thresholds); Cheap trace beats expensive sim; Held commits until verified. What cost time: two full re-sim cycles on hypotheses a cheap single-game trace would have ruled out; a trace run that reseeded the DB and landed on a non-mismatch game; initially conflating #2/#3 with the cap bug; several re-sim cycles chasing #9 fix variants that were reverted. Standing lessons: (1) Trace one game before you sim a season. (2) state.outs is the spine — both stat ledgers must reconcile to it. (3) Half the 'engine failures' were test rot (xfip→xra, local W re-derivation vs _pitcher_wl_map). (4) Instrument with an INDEPENDENT ground truth, not the suspect baseline (wrapping pa._record_out exposed all five over-charge mechanisms). (5) The sim is not reproducible across processes; the archive build (random per-game seeds, roster evolution via injuries/trades) reaches states a static loop never does. (6) Prefer an invariant-enforcing engine rule over chasing every rare input. Header metadata: Branch claude/youth-teams-league-history-nYDmy; Commits 3011c91 (career → almanac), ece4271 (invariant 8 → xRA), 2814300 (phase-outs cap + W-bound); follow-up branch claude/stat-invariants-fixes-VuqBb. Outcome: all 6 originally-failing invariants fixed. Detailed root-cause narratives for #2/#3 (four over-charge mechanisms plus joker override leak) and #9 (false-positive scored detection from stealing into an occupied base, which blindly overwrote bases[2] and silently erased the bonus runner; also corrupted ordinary runners — a hidden source of run/LOB drift) are summarized in actions; the document's investigative dead-ends (wrong hypothesis A, single-process trace narrowing, contradictory per-event instrumentation, game 1472 team 21 pid 991 cross-pitcher analysis later shown wrong) are recorded here for fidelity.
parser_notes: Document contains two superseded 'OPEN' status sections each followed by 'UPDATE (FIXED — follow-up session)' notes; statuses in the body text conflict by design. Date taken from 'Date completed:' line.
